content:
# 单篇讲义生成任务

项目仓库: mlc-ai/mlc-llm
项目名: mlc-llm
讲义目录: mlc-llm-tutorial/
代码永久链接 base: https://github.com/mlc-ai/mlc-llm/blob/a2bcc5c86678b72a86b7aadc29b643a5ce63c747/
当前 HEAD: a2bcc5c86678b72a86b7aadc29b643a5ce63c747
动作: new


---

## 项目学习路线（大纲方法论）

# 项目学习手册·大纲规划 Planner

## 角色

你是资深技术教育专家、源码阅读导师和项目学习路线设计者。

你运行在被分析项目的根目录内。

你可以使用：

- `Read`
- `Grep`
- `Glob`
- 只读 `git` 命令，例如 `git log`、`git diff`、`git show`、`git ls-files`

你不能：

- 修改源码
- 写入任何 `.md` 文件
- 输出 JSON 以外的文字

你的唯一产出是一个学习手册大纲 manifest JSON。

---

## 总目标

为当前项目规划一套「从零开始、由浅入深、结合源码和代码实践」的学习手册。

这套手册会被拆分成多篇 Markdown 讲义，后续 worker 会根据 manifest 逐篇生成。

每篇讲义应该满足：

1. 面向初学者也能读懂。
2. 只讲一个相对独立的主题。
3. 必须结合真实源码。
4. 必须包含一个可操作的代码实践任务。
5. 讲义之间有清晰依赖关系。

---

## 源码探查顺序

请按以下顺序理解项目，不要一开始就深入细节：

1. 项目说明
   - README
   - docs
   - examples
   - tutorials

2. 构建与运行方式
   - package.json
   - Cargo.toml
   - pyproject.toml
   - go.mod
   - pom.xml
   - Makefile
   - Dockerfile
   - CI 配置

3. 项目入口
   - CLI 入口
   - Web 服务入口
   - main 文件
   - library export 文件
   - examples 入口

4. 目录结构
   - src
   - lib
   - app
   - packages
   - crates
   - tests

5. 核心流程
   - 初始化流程
   - 配置加载
   - 请求/任务/数据处理主链路
   - 插件、扩展、调度、存储等核心机制

6. 测试与示例
   - 单元测试
   - 集成测试
   - benchmark
   - example usage

---

## 学习路线设计原则

请把学习手册设计成三层：

1. 入门层 beginner
   目标：让读者知道项目是什么、怎么运行、目录如何组织、核心入口在哪里。

2. 进阶层 intermediate
   目标：让读者理解主要模块、核心调用链、关键数据结构和配置机制。

3. 专家层 advanced
   目标：让读者理解扩展点、性能、并发、错误处理、测试、二次开发和架构取舍。

---

## 讲义拆分规则

- 每篇讲义只解决一个主要问题。
- 每篇讲义包含 2 到 5 个最小模块。
- 每篇讲义必须有一个 `practice_task`，描述读者要完成的代码实践。
- 每篇讲义的源码范围要适中，通常引用 1 到 6 个关键源码文件。
- 大型项目可以拆成 30 篇以上讲义；中型项目 15 到 30 篇；小型项目 8 到 15 篇。
- 不要为了数量硬拆，逻辑优先。
- 第一单元必须帮助读者从零开始，包括项目定位、运行方式、目录结构、入口文件。
- 后续单元再进入核心模块、源码机制、扩展实践。

---

## mode=full

首次生成大纲时：

1. 阅读项目关键文件。
2. 理解项目定位、技术栈、运行方式和核心模块。
3. 设计从零开始的学习路径。
4. 输出完整 manifest。
5. 所有讲义的 `action` 都必须是 `"new"`。

---

## mode=incremental

增量更新时：

1. 读取 stdin 中提供的现有 manifest。
2. 阅读项目中已有的 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 讲义目录，理解旧结构。
3. 使用：

   - `git diff previous_head..current_head`
   - `git log`
   - `git show`

   分析代码变化。

4. 输出更新后的完整 manifest。

每篇讲义的 `action` 按以下规则设置：

- `"keep"`
  代码变化不影响这篇讲义，无需重写。

- `"update"`
  小范围改动，例如函数重命名、参数调整、局部逻辑变化、链接行号变化。

- `"new"`
  新增功能、新增模块、新增重要机制，需要新增讲义。

- `"rebuild"`
  架构、主流程、公共接口、核心模型发生重大变化，旧讲义需要重建。

如果某篇旧讲义已经不再适合当前项目结构，可以从 manifest 中移除。

---

## manifest 输出格式

只输出严格 JSON，不要输出 Markdown，不要输出解释文字。

```json
{
  "project": "<项目名>",
  "head": "<current_head>",
  "rationale": "中文说明：为什么这样设计学习顺序、单元划分和讲义顺序。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "单元标题",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "讲义标题",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "本讲义要讲什么，用 1 到 3 句中文说明。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "学习目标 1",
            "学习目标 2"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "src/main.ts"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "模块 1",
            "模块 2"
          ],
          "practice_task": "本讲义对应的代码实践任务。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## 字段要求

- `project`：项目名。
- `head`：当前 git HEAD。
- `rationale`：中文，一段即可。
- `unit.id`：格式为 `u1`、`u2`、`u3`。
- `lecture.id`：格式为 `u1-l1`、`u1-l2`。
- `filename`：
  - 必须全局唯一。
  - 必须小写。
  - 使用连字符。
  - 必须以 `.md` 结尾。
  - 文件位于 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 目录下。
- `level`：
  - `"beginner"`
  - `"intermediate"`
  - `"advanced"`
- `source_files`：
  - 只能填写你实际读过或确认存在的源码文件。
  - 不要编造文件路径。
- `depends_on`：
  - 使用讲义 id。
  - 没有依赖时填写空数组。
- `action`：
  - full 模式全部为 `"new"`。
  - incremental 模式使用 `"keep"`、`"update"`、`"new"`、`"rebuild"`。

---

## 重要约束

- 只基于真实源码和项目文件规划。
- 不确定的地方写 `"待确认"`。
- 不要编造不存在的模块、命令、接口或源码文件。
- 输出必须是合法 JSON。


## 完整大纲

```json
{
  "project": "mlc-llm",
  "rationale": "MLC LLM 是一个「ML 编译器 + 高性能推理引擎」的大型多语言项目，核心链路是 convert_weight → gen_config → compile（Python/TVM Relax）→ chat/serve（C++ ThreadedEngine + Python REST）。学习顺序据此分三层铺开：入门层（U1–U2）先建立项目定位、目录结构、构建方式、CLI 子命令与端到端工作流的整体认知；进阶层（U3–U7、U11）拆解五大相对独立子系统——模型定义（Model 注册表 + Relax nn）、权重加载与映射、量化、对话模板与 OpenAI 协议、编译接口与 pass 流水线，以及 Python 引擎与 REST 服务，使读者理解「一个 HF 模型如何变成可运行产物」的全过程；专家层（U8–U10、U12）深入编译优化 pass、C++ 推理引擎的事件-动作循环、分页 KV 缓存/前缀缓存（Radix Tree）/采样/推测解码，以及多 GPU、微服务路由、分离式推理与多端部署等扩展点。讲义按「单主题、2–5 个最小模块、1 个实践任务、引用真实源码」拆分，单元间通过 depends_on 形成清晰依赖（编译产物驱动引擎、模型定义被加载/量化/编译依赖、协议贯穿服务端）。共 12 单元 38 篇，匹配项目规模。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "走进 MLC LLM：定位、结构与工作流",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "项目总览：ML 编译驱动的通用 LLM 部署引擎",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "介绍 MLC LLM 是什么、解决什么问题：基于 TVM/Relax 的机器学习编译器与跨平台 LLM 推理引擎，支持 CUDA/Metal/Vulkan/WebGPU 等。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 MLC LLM 的定位：编译器 + 部署引擎双重身份",
            "了解支持的平台与后端（GPU/移动端/Web）",
            "认识 MLCEngine 提供 OpenAI 兼容 API 的统一抽象"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "docs/get_started/introduction.rst",
            "docs/index.rst"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "项目使命与定位",
            "支持的平台/后端矩阵",
            "MLCEngine 与 OpenAI 兼容 API"
          ],
          "practice_task": "阅读 README 与 introduction 文档，用一句话写出 MLC LLM 的定位，并列举它至少支持的三种目标后端；在本地运行 python -c \"import mlc_llm; print(mlc_llm.__path__)\" 验证安装。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l2",
          "title": "仓库目录结构与多语言布局",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u1-l2-repository-structure.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解仓库顶层目录划分：python/（编译器与 CLI）、cpp/（推理引擎与 json_ffi）、android/、ios、docs/、examples/、3rdparty/（tvm、tokenizers-cpp、xgrammar）等。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "掌握顶层目录与各自职责",
            "区分编译期（Python）与运行期（C++）代码",
            "认识第三方依赖 tvm/tokenizers-cpp/xgrammar 的作用"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "CMakeLists.txt",
            "python/setup.py",
            "docs/index.rst"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "顶层目录职责映射",
            "Python 包与 C++ 库的边界",
            "第三方依赖（TVM 等）"
          ],
          "practice_task": "对照 git ls-files 输出，绘制一张顶层目录思维导图，标注每个目录属于「编译」「引擎」「部署」「文档」哪一类，并指出 C++ 入口在 cpp/serve 与 cpp/json_ffi。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l3",
          "title": "安装、构建与快速运行",
          "order": 3,
          "filename": "u1-l3-install-and-quickstart.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解如何安装 mlc_llm Python 包、为何编译模型还需要 TVM，以及三种快速运行方式：chat CLI、Python MLCEngine、REST 服务器。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "完成 mlc_llm 与 tvm 的安装验证",
            "会用 chat CLI 加载 HF:// MLC 模型对话",
            "会用 Python MLCEngine 与 REST 服务器"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "docs/get_started/quick_start.rst",
            "examples/python/sample_mlc_engine.py",
            "python/mlc_llm/libinfo.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "安装与验证",
            "chat CLI 一键运行",
            "Python API 与 REST 用法"
          ],
          "practice_task": "运行 examples/python/sample_mlc_engine.py，使用 MLCEngine 对 Llama-3-8B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC 发起一次流式 chat completion，并打印每秒 token 数（/stats）。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l2"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l4",
          "title": "端到端工作流与模型产物",
          "order": 4,
          "filename": "u1-l4-workflow-and-artifacts.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "串联完整工作流 convert_weight → gen_config → compile → serve，并解释三大产物：MLC 权重、model library、mlc-chat-config.json。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解四步工作流与各步产物",
            "区分 model weights / model lib / chat config",
            "知道何时可跳过显式 compile（JIT）"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "docs/compilation/compile_models.rst",
            "docs/get_started/introduction.rst",
            "python/mlc_llm/protocol/mlc_chat_config.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "四步工作流",
            "三类模型产物",
            "JIT 编译兜底机制"
          ],
          "practice_task": "浏览 mlc_chat_config.py 中的 MLCChatConfig 字段，列出至少 8 个关键字段（如 model_lib、quantization、conv_template、context_window_size），并说明它们分别由哪一步工作流生成。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l3"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "u2",
      "title": "命令行与入口：mlc_llm CLI",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 2,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u2-l1",
          "title": "CLI 总入口与子命令分发",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u2-l1-cli-entrypoint.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "解析 python/mlc_llm/__main__.py 如何把单一入口分发到 compile/convert_weight/gen_config/chat/serve/package/calibrate/router 等子命令。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 mlc_llm 的入口与子命令体系",
            "掌握 CLI 层（cli/）与接口层（interface/）的分层",
            "知道 package.json console_scripts 注册方式"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "python/mlc_llm/__main__.py",
            "python/mlc_llm/__init__.py",
            "python/setup.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "子命令分发机制",
            "cli/ 与 interface/ 分层",
            "入口点注册"
          ],
          "practice_task": "运行 mlc_llm --help，列出全部子命令；随后追踪某一个子命令（如 compile）从 __main__.py 到 cli/compile.py 再到 interface/compile.py 的调用链并画出来。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l4"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l2",
          "title": "模型编译三件套：convert_weight / gen_config / compile",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u2-l2-compile-trio-commands.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 cli/convert_weight.py、cli/gen_config.py、cli/compile.py 三个命令的参数与作用，以及它们如何把 HF 模型变成 MLC 可运行产物。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "会用三条命令完成模型准备",
            "理解 --quantization、--model-type、--target 等关键参数",
            "认识参数自动探测（detect_*）"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "python/mlc_llm/cli/convert_weight.py",
            "python/mlc_llm/cli/gen_config.py",
            "python/mlc_llm/cli/compile.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "convert_weight 命令",
            "gen_config 命令",
            "compile 命令与 target"
          ],
          "practice_task": "选一个小模型（如 RedPajama-INCITE-Chat-3B-v1），依次运行 convert_weight、gen_config、compile，记录每一步产出的文件，并用 file 命令查看产出的 .so/.tar 格式。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l3",
          "title": "运行入口：chat / serve / package",
          "order": 3,
          "filename": "u2-l3-run-commands.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 cli/chat.py（交互对话）、cli/serve.py（启动 REST 服务器）、cli/package.py（打包 iOS/Android 库与权重）三类运行命令。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "会用 chat CLI 交互并使用 /set、/stats 等指令",
            "会用 serve 启动 OpenAI 兼容服务器",
            "了解 package 打包移动端产物的用途"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "python/mlc_llm/cli/chat.py",
            "python/mlc_llm/cli/serve.py",
            "python/mlc_llm/cli/package.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "chat CLI 与特殊指令",
            "serve 启动参数",
            "package 打包流程"
          ],
          "practice_task": "用 mlc_llm serve 启动一个服务器，再用 curl 向 /v1/chat/completions 发送请求；接着尝试 /set temperature=0.5 在 chat CLI 中覆盖生成参数。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "u3",
      "title": "模型定义系统：用 Relax 描述架构",
      "level": "intermediate",
      "order": 3,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u3-l1",
          "title": "Model 注册表：如何描述一个模型架构",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u3-l1-model-registry.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "解析 model/model.py 中 Model dataclass 与 MODELS 注册表：name、config、model、source、quantize、model_task 等字段如何把架构、配置、加载、量化绑定在一起。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 Model dataclass 的字段含义",
            "掌握 MODELS 注册表的注册模式",
            "区分 chat 与 embedding 两种 model_task"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "python/mlc_llm/model/model.py",
            "python/mlc_llm/model/model_preset.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "Model dataclass 字段",
            "MODELS 注册表",
            "source/quantize 映射字典"
          ],
          "practice_task": "在 model.py 的 MODELS 中挑两个模型（如 llama 与 mistral），对比它们的 source 与 quantize 配置差异，并解释 supports_awq、supports_per_tensor 等开关的作用。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l4"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u3-l2",
          "title": "用 TVM Relax nn 编写模型",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u3-l2-relax-nn-model.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "以 llama_model.py 为例，讲解如何用 tvm.relax.frontend.nn（nn.Module、nn.Linear、nn.RMSNorm）定义 LlamaConfig/LlamaAttention/LlamaDecoderLayer/LlamaForCausalLM，以及 prefill/decode/batch_verify 等方法与 get_default_spec。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "掌握 Relax nn.Module 定义模型的方式",
            "理解 prefill/decode/verify 等执行入口",
            "认识 get_default_spec 与张量接口约定"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "python/mlc_llm/model/llama/llama_model.py",
            "python/mlc_llm/model/model_utils.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "Relax nn 模型结构",
            "注意力与 FFN 层",
            "执行入口与 spec"
          ],
          "practice_task": "阅读 LlamaForCausalLM，找到 prefill、decode、create_paged_kv_cache、get_logits 四个方法，分别用一句话描述其输入输出；再说明 RoPE 配置在 create_paged_kv_cache 中如何传入。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u3-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u3-l3",
```

## 前置讲义摘要（你必须基于这些已建立的认知继续，不要重复，要承接）

### u1-l1 项目总览：ML 编译驱动的通用 LLM 部署引擎

本讲是 MLC LLM 学习手册的开篇，建立全局心智模型，只读不写代码。核心定位：MLC LLM 是「基于 ML 编译的通用 LLM 部署引擎」，兼具**编译器**（把模型优化成硬件专用代码）与**部署引擎**（驱动推理并对外服务）两重身份，使命是让任意开源 LLM 原生部署到从手机、PC、服务器到浏览器的任意平台，不挑硬件、不绑云。其工作分三阶段：**下载预量化权重 → 编译模型库（用 Apache TVM，仅首次，结果缓存）→ 启动运行时**。跨平台需区分**平台**（OS/运行环境）与**后端**（CUDA、ROCm、Metal、Vulkan、WebGPU/WASM、OpenCL 等计算驱动），切换只需 `--device`，唯一硬约束是显存「装得下、算得动」。对外抽象为 **MLCEngine**——一套跨平台/后端的统一引擎，提供 **OpenAI 兼容 API**，可经 chat CLI、Python（同步 `MLCEngine`/异步 `AsyncMLCEngine`）、REST 服务器三种入口访问，背后是同一套引擎与编译器。验证安装用 `import mlc_llm; print(mlc_llm.__path__)`。后续 u1-l2 将进入仓库目录结构与源码。
### u1-l2 仓库目录结构与多语言布局

本讲是纯阅读理解型讲义，为 MLC LLM 仓库建立「目录地图」，覆盖三个最小模块：顶层目录职责映射、Python 包与 C++ 库的边界、第三方依赖。核心结论：顶层目录可按编译、引擎、部署、文档、依赖、工程六类贴标签——`python/` 同时承载编译器、CLI 与引擎封装，`cpp/` 是高性能推理引擎，`android/ios/web` 是各平台部署，`3rdparty/` 是依赖地基。关键边界判断：编译期逻辑（model/quantization/compiler_pass/loader/interface）几乎全在 Python 侧，运行期快路径在 C++ 侧（`cpp/serve/`），两者经 **JSON FFI** 衔接——Python 把请求序列化成 JSON 调进 C++，`MLCEngine` 只是这层边界的薄封装。引入的关键术语：编译期/运行期、JSON FFI、MLCEngine、console_scripts 入口点、scikit-build-core（在 pip install 时调 CMake 一并构建 C++ 产物 `libmlc_llm.so`）、git submodule。三大依赖地基：`tvm`（编译能力来源，用的是 `mlc-ai/relax` 定制 fork）、`tokenizers-cpp`（文本↔token 转换）、`xgrammar`（结构化约束解码）。推理引擎心脏在 `cpp/serve/threaded_engine.cc`，JSON 桥在 `cpp/json_ffi/`。
### u1-l3 安装、构建与快速运行

本讲把前两讲的「读」转化为「跑」，回答「如何安装 mlc_llm 并最快地与真实大模型对话一次」。核心内容：一是两种安装方式——预编译 wheel（快）与源码编译（灵活），验证分 Python 层（`import mlc_llm`）和 C++ 层（找到 `libmlc_llm.so`），由 `libinfo.py` 跨平台定位动态库。关键结论：跑模型只需随包附带的 TVM runtime，无需完整 TVM 编译器，后者仅用于编译自己的模型。二是三种运行入口对应三种 mode：chat CLI（`interactive`，走 `JSONFFIEngine`）、Python `MLCEngine`（`local`，走 `MLCEngineBase`）、REST serve（`server`，`AsyncMLCEngine`+FastAPI）。关键术语：`HF://` 前缀、JSON FFI 桥、两扇「门」（`CreateJSONFFIEngine` 与 `create_threaded_engine` 最终都创建同一 C++ ThreadedEngine）、速度统计同源于 `usage.extra` 的 `prefill/decode_tokens_per_s`。

---

## 本讲义规格

- id: u1-l4
- 文件名: u1-l4-workflow-and-artifacts.md
- 写入路径: mlc-llm-tutorial/u1-l4-workflow-and-artifacts.md
- 标题: 端到端工作流与模型产物
- 学习阶段: beginner
- 主题: 串联完整工作流 convert_weight → gen_config → compile → serve，并解释三大产物：MLC 权重、model library、mlc-chat-config.json。
- 学习目标: 理解四步工作流与各步产物；区分 model weights / model lib / chat config；知道何时可跳过显式 compile（JIT）
- 应覆盖的最小模块: 四步工作流，三类模型产物，JIT 编译兜底机制
- 关键源码: docs/compilation/compile_models.rst，docs/get_started/introduction.rst，python/mlc_llm/protocol/mlc_chat_config.py
- 代码实践任务: 浏览 mlc_chat_config.py 中的 MLCChatConfig 字段，列出至少 8 个关键字段（如 model_lib、quantization、conv_template、context_window_size），并说明它们分别由哪一步工作流生成。
- 依赖讲义: u1-l3

---

## 任务

请按照 worker prompt 生成这一篇讲义。

要求：

1. 只生成 `mlc-llm-tutorial/u1-l4-workflow-and-artifacts.md` 这一个文件。
2. 必须结合真实源码。
3. 必须包含代码实践。
4. 必须使用永久链接引用源码。
5. new/rebuild 模式下从零写入。
6. update 模式下先读取旧文件，再结合 diff 更新。
7. 不要修改源码。
8. 不要写其他文件。

完成后，用一句话总结本讲义覆盖的最小模块。

当前 HEAD: a2bcc5c86678b72a86b7aadc29b643a5ce63c747
动作: new


---

## 项目学习路线（大纲方法论）

# 项目学习手册·大纲规划 Planner

## 角色

你是资深技术教育专家、源码阅读导师和项目学习路线设计者。

你运行在被分析项目的根目录内。

你可以使用：

- `Read`
- `Grep`
- `Glob`
- 只读 `git` 命令，例如 `git log`、`git diff`、`git show`、`git ls-files`

你不能：

- 修改源码
- 写入任何 `.md` 文件
- 输出 JSON 以外的文字

你的唯一产出是一个学习手册大纲 manifest JSON。

---

## 总目标

为当前项目规划一套「从零开始、由浅入深、结合源码和代码实践」的学习手册。

这套手册会被拆分成多篇 Markdown 讲义，后续 worker 会根据 manifest 逐篇生成。

每篇讲义应该满足：

1. 面向初学者也能读懂。
2. 只讲一个相对独立的主题。
3. 必须结合真实源码。
4. 必须包含一个可操作的代码实践任务。
5. 讲义之间有清晰依赖关系。

---

## 源码探查顺序

请按以下顺序理解项目，不要一开始就深入细节：

1. 项目说明
   - README
   - docs
   - examples
   - tutorials

2. 构建与运行方式
   - package.json
   - Cargo.toml
   - pyproject.toml
   - go.mod
   - pom.xml
   - Makefile
   - Dockerfile
   - CI 配置

3. 项目入口
   - CLI 入口
   - Web 服务入口
   - main 文件
   - library export 文件
   - examples 入口

4. 目录结构
   - src
   - lib
   - app
   - packages
   - crates
   - tests

5. 核心流程
   - 初始化流程
   - 配置加载
   - 请求/任务/数据处理主链路
   - 插件、扩展、调度、存储等核心机制

6. 测试与示例
   - 单元测试
   - 集成测试
   - benchmark
   - example usage

---

## 学习路线设计原则

请把学习手册设计成三层：

1. 入门层 beginner
   目标：让读者知道项目是什么、怎么运行、目录如何组织、核心入口在哪里。

2. 进阶层 intermediate
   目标：让读者理解主要模块、核心调用链、关键数据结构和配置机制。

3. 专家层 advanced
   目标：让读者理解扩展点、性能、并发、错误处理、测试、二次开发和架构取舍。

---

## 讲义拆分规则

- 每篇讲义只解决一个主要问题。
- 每篇讲义包含 2 到 5 个最小模块。
- 每篇讲义必须有一个 `practice_task`，描述读者要完成的代码实践。
- 每篇讲义的源码范围要适中，通常引用 1 到 6 个关键源码文件。
- 大型项目可以拆成 30 篇以上讲义；中型项目 15 到 30 篇；小型项目 8 到 15 篇。
- 不要为了数量硬拆，逻辑优先。
- 第一单元必须帮助读者从零开始，包括项目定位、运行方式、目录结构、入口文件。
- 后续单元再进入核心模块、源码机制、扩展实践。

---

## mode=full

首次生成大纲时：

1. 阅读项目关键文件。
2. 理解项目定位、技术栈、运行方式和核心模块。
3. 设计从零开始的学习路径。
4. 输出完整 manifest。
5. 所有讲义的 `action` 都必须是 `"new"`。

---

## mode=incremental

增量更新时：

1. 读取 stdin 中提供的现有 manifest。
2. 阅读项目中已有的 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 讲义目录，理解旧结构。
3. 使用：

   - `git diff previous_head..current_head`
   - `git log`
   - `git show`

   分析代码变化。

4. 输出更新后的完整 manifest。

每篇讲义的 `action` 按以下规则设置：

- `"keep"`
  代码变化不影响这篇讲义，无需重写。

- `"update"`
  小范围改动，例如函数重命名、参数调整、局部逻辑变化、链接行号变化。

- `"new"`
  新增功能、新增模块、新增重要机制，需要新增讲义。

- `"rebuild"`
  架构、主流程、公共接口、核心模型发生重大变化，旧讲义需要重建。

如果某篇旧讲义已经不再适合当前项目结构，可以从 manifest 中移除。

---

## manifest 输出格式

只输出严格 JSON，不要输出 Markdown，不要输出解释文字。

```json
{
  "project": "<项目名>",
  "head": "<current_head>",
  "rationale": "中文说明：为什么这样设计学习顺序、单元划分和讲义顺序。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "单元标题",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "讲义标题",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "本讲义要讲什么，用 1 到 3 句中文说明。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "学习目标 1",
            "学习目标 2"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "src/main.ts"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "模块 1",
            "模块 2"
          ],
          "practice_task": "本讲义对应的代码实践任务。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## 字段要求

- `project`：项目名。
- `head`：当前 git HEAD。
- `rationale`：中文，一段即可。
- `unit.id`：格式为 `u1`、`u2`、`u3`。
- `lecture.id`：格式为 `u1-l1`、`u1-l2`。
- `filename`：
  - 必须全局唯一。
  - 必须小写。
  - 使用连字符。
  - 必须以 `.md` 结尾。
  - 文件位于 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 目录下。
- `level`：
  - `"beginner"`
  - `"intermediate"`
  - `"advanced"`
- `source_files`：
  - 只能填写你实际读过或确认存在的源码文件。
  - 不要编造文件路径。
- `depends_on`：
  - 使用讲义 id。
  - 没有依赖时填写空数组。
- `action`：
  - full 模式全部为 `"new"`。
  - incremental 模式使用 `"keep"`、`"update"`、`"new"`、`"rebuild"`。

---

## 重要约束

- 只基于真实源码和项目文件规划。
- 不确定的地方写 `"待确认"`。
- 不要编造不存在的模块、命令、接口或源码文件。
- 输出必须是合法 JSON。

## 前置讲义摘要（你必须基于这些已建立的认知继续，不要重复，要承接）

### u1-l1 项目总览：ML 编译驱动的通用 LLM 部署引擎

本讲是 MLC LLM 学习手册的开篇，建立全局心智模型，只读不写代码。核心定位：MLC LLM 是「基于 ML 编译的通用 LLM 部署引擎」，兼具**编译器**（把模型优化成硬件专用代码）与**部署引擎**（驱动推理并对外服务）两重身份，使命是让任意开源 LLM 原生部署到从手机、PC、服务器到浏览器的任意平台，不挑硬件、不绑云。其工作分三阶段：**下载预量化权重 → 编译模型库（用 Apache TVM，仅首次，结果缓存）→ 启动运行时**。跨平台需区分**平台**（OS/运行环境）与**后端**（CUDA、ROCm、Metal、Vulkan、WebGPU/WASM、OpenCL 等计算驱动），切换只需 `--device`，唯一硬约束是显存「装得下、算得动」。对外抽象为 **MLCEngine**——一套跨平台/后端的统一引擎，提供 **OpenAI 兼容 API**，可经 chat CLI、Python（同步 `MLCEngine`/异步 `AsyncMLCEngine`）、REST 服务器三种入口访问，背后是同一套引擎与编译器。验证安装用 `import mlc_llm; print(mlc_llm.__path__)`。后续 u1-l2 将进入仓库目录结构与源码。
### u1-l2 仓库目录结构与多语言布局

本讲是纯阅读理解型讲义，为 MLC LLM 仓库建立「目录地图」，覆盖三个最小模块：顶层目录职责映射、Python 包与 C++ 库的边界、第三方依赖。核心结论：顶层目录可按编译、引擎、部署、文档、依赖、工程六类贴标签——`python/` 同时承载编译器、CLI 与引擎封装，`cpp/` 是高性能推理引擎，`android/ios/web` 是各平台部署，`3rdparty/` 是依赖地基。关键边界判断：编译期逻辑（model/quantization/compiler_pass/loader/interface）几乎全在 Python 侧，运行期快路径在 C++ 侧（`cpp/serve/`），两者经 **JSON FFI** 衔接——Python 把请求序列化成 JSON 调进 C++，`MLCEngine` 只是这层边界的薄封装。引入的关键术语：编译期/运行期、JSON FFI、MLCEngine、console_scripts 入口点、scikit-build-core（在 pip install 时调 CMake 一并构建 C++ 产物 `libmlc_llm.so`）、git submodule。三大依赖地基：`tvm`（编译能力来源，用的是 `mlc-ai/relax` 定制 fork）、`tokenizers-cpp`（文本↔token 转换）、`xgrammar`（结构化约束解码）。推理引擎心脏在 `cpp/serve/threaded_engine.cc`，JSON 桥在 `cpp/json_ffi/`。
### u1-l3 安装、构建与快速运行

本讲把前两讲的「读」转化为「跑」，回答「如何安装 mlc_llm 并最快地与真实大模型对话一次」。核心内容：一是两种安装方式——预编译 wheel（快）与源码编译（灵活），验证分 Python 层（`import mlc_llm`）和 C++ 层（找到 `libmlc_llm.so`），由 `libinfo.py` 跨平台定位动态库。关键结论：跑模型只需随包附带的 TVM runtime，无需完整 TVM 编译器，后者仅用于编译自己的模型。二是三种运行入口对应三种 mode：chat CLI（`interactive`，走 `JSONFFIEngine`）、Python `MLCEngine`（`local`，走 `MLCEngineBase`）、REST serve（`server`，`AsyncMLCEngine`+FastAPI）。关键术语：`HF://` 前缀、JSON FFI 桥、两扇「门」（`CreateJSONFFIEngine` 与 `create_threaded_engine` 最终都创建同一 C++ ThreadedEngine）、速度统计同源于 `usage.extra` 的 `prefill/decode_tokens_per_s`。

"""Schema for mlc-chat-config"""

from typing import Any, Dict, List, Literal, Optional, Union  # noqa: UP035

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

from mlc_llm.support.constants import MLC_CHAT_CONFIG_VERSION

from .conversation_protocol import Conversation

MLC_CHAT_SYSTEM_DEFAULT = {
    "pad_token_id": 0,
    "bos_token_id": 1,
    "eos_token_id": 2,
    "temperature": 1.0,
    "presence_penalty": 0.0,
    "frequency_penalty": 0.0,
    "repetition_penalty": 1.0,
    "top_p": 1.0,
}
"""system default values."""


class MLCChatConfig(BaseModel):
    """Fields in the dumped `mlc-chat-config.json` file."""

    # Version control
    version: str = MLC_CHAT_CONFIG_VERSION

    # use alias to avoid protected namespace conflict with pydantic
    field_model_type: str = Field(alias="model_type")
    quantization: str
    # use alias to avoid protected namespace conflict with pydantic
    field_model_config: Dict[str, Any] = Field(alias="model_config")  # noqa: UP006
    vocab_size: int
    context_window_size: int
    sliding_window_size: int
    prefill_chunk_size: int
    attention_sink_size: int
    tensor_parallel_shards: int
    pipeline_parallel_stages: int = 1
    # Configuration of text generation
    active_vocab_size: int = None
    temperature: Optional[float] = None
    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None
    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None
    repetition_penalty: Optional[float] = None
    top_p: Optional[float] = None
    # Tokenizer configuration
    tokenizer_files: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list)  # noqa: UP006
    # The content of tokenizer.TokenizerInfo
    tokenizer_info: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)  # noqa: UP006
    # conversation template
    conv_template: Conversation
    # extra fields from generation_config.json
    # NOTE: they are not being used for now in MLCEngine
    # but we keep them for book-keep purposes
    pad_token_id: Optional[int] = None
    bos_token_id: Optional[int] = None
    eos_token_id: Optional[Union[int, List[int]]] = None  # noqa: UP006

    field_model_task: Literal["chat", "embedding"] = Field(default="chat", alias="model_task")
    embedding_metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None  # noqa: UP006

    def get_system_defaults_for_missing_fields(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:  # noqa: UP006
        """Apply system default value for fields that are None

        Note
        ----
        We implement default setting in this way so we can lazily create
        MLCChatConfig, override its optional values then
        apply_system_defaults in the end.
        """
        res = {}
        for key, value in MLC_CHAT_SYSTEM_DEFAULT.items():
            if getattr(self, key) is None:
                res[key] = value
        return res

1	.. _introduction-to-mlc-llm:
2	
3	Introduction to MLC LLM
4	=======================
5	
6	.. contents:: Table of Contents
7	    :local:
8	    :depth: 2
9	
10	MLC LLM is a machine learning compiler and high-performance deployment
11	engine for large language models.  The mission of this project is to enable everyone to develop,
12	optimize, and deploy AI models natively on everyone's platforms. 
13	
14	This page is a quick tutorial to introduce how to try out MLC LLM, and the steps to
15	deploy your own models with MLC LLM.
16	
17	Installation
18	------------
19	
20	:ref:`MLC LLM <install-mlc-packages>` is available via pip.
21	It is always recommended to install it in an isolated conda virtual environment.
22	
23	To verify the installation, activate your virtual environment, run
24	
25	.. code:: bash
26	
27	  python -c "import mlc_llm; print(mlc_llm.__path__)"
28	
29	You are expected to see the installation path of MLC LLM Python package.
30	
31	
32	Chat CLI
33	--------
34	
35	As the first example, we try out the chat CLI in MLC LLM with 4-bit quantized 8B Llama-3 model.
36	You can run MLC chat through a one-liner command:
37	
38	.. code:: bash
39	
40	    mlc_llm chat HF://mlc-ai/Llama-3-8B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC
41	
42	It may take 1-2 minutes for the first time running this command.
43	After waiting, this command launch a chat interface where you can enter your prompt and chat with the model.
44	
45	.. code::
46	
47	  You can use the following special commands:
48	  /help               print the special commands
49	  /exit               quit the cli
50	  /stats              print out the latest stats (token/sec)
51	  /reset              restart a fresh chat
52	  /set [overrides]    override settings in the generation config. For example,
53	                        `/set temperature=0.5;max_gen_len=100;stop=end,stop`
54	                        Note: Separate stop words in the `stop` option with commas (,).
55	  Multi-line input: Use escape+enter to start a new line.
56	
57	  user: What's the meaning of life
58	  assistant:
59	  What a profound and intriguing question! While there's no one definitive answer, I'd be happy to help you explore some perspectives on the meaning of life.
60	
61	  The concept of the meaning of life has been debated and...
62	
63	
64	The figure below shows what run under the hood of this chat CLI command.
65	For the first time running the command, there are three major phases.
66	
67	- **Phase 1. Pre-quantized weight download.** This phase automatically downloads pre-quantized Llama-3 model from `Hugging Face <https://huggingface.co/mlc-ai/Llama-3-8B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC>`_ and saves it to your local cache directory.
68	- **Phase 2. Model compilation.** This phase automatically optimizes the Llama-3 model to accelerate model inference on GPU with techniques of machine learning compilation in `Apache TVM <https://llm.mlc.ai/docs/install/tvm.html>`_ compiler, and generate the binary model library that enables the execution language models on your local GPU.
69	- **Phase 3. Chat runtime.** This phase consumes the model library built in phase 2 and the model weights downloaded in phase 1, launches a platform-native chat runtime to drive the execution of Llama-3 model.
70	
71	We cache the pre-quantized model weights and compiled model library locally.
72	Therefore, phase 1 and 2 will only execute **once** over multiple runs.
73	
74	.. figure:: /_static/img/project-workflow.svg
75	  :width: 700
76	  :align: center
77	  :alt: Project Workflow
78	
79	  Workflow in MLC LLM
80	
81	.. note::
82	
83	  If you want to enable tensor parallelism to run LLMs on multiple GPUs,
84	  please specify argument ``--overrides "tensor_parallel_shards=$NGPU"``.
85	  For example,
86	
87	  .. code:: shell
88	
89	    mlc_llm chat HF://mlc-ai/Llama-3-8B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC --overrides "tensor_parallel_shards=2"
90	
91	.. _introduction-to-mlc-llm-python-api:
92	
93	Python API
94	----------
95	
96	In the second example, we run the Llama-3 model with the chat completion Python API of MLC LLM.
97	You can save the code below into a Python file and run it.
98	
99	.. code:: python
100	
101	  from mlc_llm import MLCEngine
102	
103	  # Create engine
104	  model = "HF://mlc-ai/Llama-3-8B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC"
105	  engine = MLCEngine(model)
106	
107	  # Run chat completion in OpenAI API.
108	  for response in engine.chat.completions.create(
109	      messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What is the meaning of life?"}],
110	      model=model,
111	      stream=True,
112	  ):
113	      for choice in response.choices:
114	          print(choice.delta.content, end="", flush=True)
115	  print("\n")
116	
117	  engine.terminate()
118	
119	.. figure:: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mlc-ai/web-data/main/images/mlc-llm/tutorials/python-engine-api.jpg
120	  :width: 500
121	  :align: center
122	
123	  MLC LLM Python API
124	
125	This code example first creates an :class:`mlc_llm.MLCEngine` instance with the 4-bit quantized Llama-3 model.
126	**We design the Python API** :class:`mlc_llm.MLCEngine` **to align with OpenAI API**,
127	which means you can use :class:`mlc_llm.MLCEngine` in the same way of using
128	`OpenAI's Python package <https://github.com/openai/openai-python?tab=readme-ov-file#usage>`_
129	for both synchronous and asynchronous generation.
130	
131	In this code example, we use the synchronous chat completion interface and iterate over
132	all the stream responses.
133	If you want to run without streaming, you can run
134	
135	.. code:: python
136	
137	  response = engine.chat.completions.create(
138	      messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What is the meaning of life?"}],
139	      model=model,
140	      stream=False,
141	  )
142	  print(response)
143	
144	You can also try different arguments supported in `OpenAI chat completion API <https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create>`_.
145	If you would like to do concurrent asynchronous generation, you can use :class:`mlc_llm.AsyncMLCEngine` instead.
146	
147	.. note::
148	
149	  If you want to enable tensor parallelism to run LLMs on multiple GPUs,
150	  please specify argument ``model_config_overrides`` in MLCEngine constructor.
151	  For example,
152	
153	  .. code:: python
154	
155	    from mlc_llm import MLCEngine
156	    from mlc_llm.serve.config import EngineConfig
157	
158	    model = "HF://mlc-ai/Llama-3-8B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC"
159	    engine = MLCEngine(
160	        model,
161	        engine_config=EngineConfig(tensor_parallel_shards=2),
162	    )
163	
164	
165	REST Server
166	-----------
167	
168	For the third example, we launch a REST server to serve the 4-bit quantized Llama-3 model
169	for OpenAI chat completion requests. The server can be launched in command line with
170	
171	.. code:: bash
172	
173	  mlc_llm serve HF://mlc-ai/Llama-3-8B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC
174	
175	The server is hooked at ``http://127.0.0.1:8000`` by default, and you can use ``--host`` and ``--port``
176	to set a different host and port.
177	When the server is ready (showing ``INFO: Uvicorn running on http://127.0.0.1:8000 (Press CTRL+C to quit)``),
178	we can open a new shell and send a cURL request via the following command:
179	
180	.. code:: bash
181	
182	  curl -X POST \
183	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
184	    -d '{
185	          "model": "HF://mlc-ai/Llama-3-8B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC",
186	          "messages": [
187	              {"role": "user", "content": "Hello! Our project is MLC LLM. What is the name of our project?"}
188	          ]
189	    }' \
190	    http://127.0.0.1:8000/v1/chat/completions
191	
192	The server will process this request and send back the response.
193	Similar to :ref:`introduction-to-mlc-llm-python-api`, you can pass argument ``"stream": true``
194	to request for stream responses.
195	
196	.. note::
197	
198	  If you want to enable tensor parallelism to run LLMs on multiple GPUs,
199	  please specify argument ``--overrides "tensor_parallel_shards=$NGPU"``.
200	  For example,
201	
202	  .. code:: shell
203	
204	    mlc_llm serve HF://mlc-ai/Llama-3-8B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC --overrides "tensor_parallel_shards=2"
205	
206	.. _introduction-deploy-your-own-model:
207	
208	Deploy Your Own Model
209	---------------------
210	
211	So far we have been using pre-converted models weights from Hugging Face.
212	This section introduces the core workflow regarding how you can *run your own models with MLC LLM*.
213	
214	We use the `Phi-2 <https://huggingface.co/microsoft/phi-2>`_ as the example model.
215	Assuming the Phi-2 model is downloaded and placed under ``models/phi-2``,
216	there are two major steps to prepare your own models.
217	
218	- **Step 1. Generate MLC config.** The first step is to generate the configuration file of MLC LLM.
219	
220	  .. code:: bash
221	
222	    export LOCAL_MODEL_PATH=models/phi-2   # The path where the model resides locally.
223	    export MLC_MODEL_PATH=dist/phi-2-MLC/  # The path where to place the model processed by MLC.
224	    export QUANTIZATION=q0f16              # The choice of quantization.
225	    export CONV_TEMPLATE=phi-2             # The choice of conversation template.
226	    mlc_llm gen_config $LOCAL_MODEL_PATH \
227	        --quantization $QUANTIZATION \
228	        --conv-template $CONV_TEMPLATE \
229	        -o $MLC_MODEL_PATH
230	
231	  The config generation command takes in the local model path, the target path of MLC output,
232	  the conversation template name in MLC and the quantization name in MLC.
233	  Here the quantization ``q0f16`` means float16 without quantization,
234	  and the conversation template ``phi-2`` is the Phi-2 model's template in MLC.
235	
236	  If you want to enable tensor parallelism on multiple GPUs, add argument
237	  ``--tensor-parallel-shards $NGPU`` to the config generation command.
238	
239	  - `The full list of supported quantization in MLC <https://github.com/mlc-ai/mlc-llm/blob/main/python/mlc_llm/quantization/quantization.py#L29>`_. You can try different quantization methods with MLC LLM. Typical quantization methods are ``q4f16_1`` for 4-bit group quantization, ``q4f16_ft`` for 4-bit FasterTransformer format quantization.
240	  - `The full list of conversation template in MLC <https://github.com/mlc-ai/mlc-llm/blob/main/python/mlc_llm/interface/gen_config.py#L276>`_.
241	
242	- **Step 2. Convert model weights.** In this step, we convert the model weights to MLC format.
243	
244	  .. code:: bash
245	
246	    mlc_llm convert_weight $LOCAL_MODEL_PATH \
247	      --quantization $QUANTIZATION \
248	      -o $MLC_MODEL_PATH
249	
250	  This step consumes the raw model weights and converts them to for MLC format.
251	  The converted weights will be stored under ``$MLC_MODEL_PATH``,
252	  which is the same directory where the config file generated in Step 1 resides.
253	
254	Now, we can try to run your own model with chat CLI:
255	
256	.. code:: bash
257	
258	  mlc_llm chat $MLC_MODEL_PATH
259	
260	For the first run, model compilation will be triggered automatically to optimize the
261	model for GPU accelerate and generate the binary model library.
262	The chat interface will be displayed after model JIT compilation finishes.
263	You can also use this model in Python API, MLC serve and other use scenarios.
264	
265	(Optional) Compile Model Library
266	~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
267	
268	In previous sections, model libraries are compiled when the :class:`mlc_llm.MLCEngine` launches,
269	which is what we call "JIT (Just-in-Time) model compilation".
270	In some cases, it is beneficial to explicitly compile the model libraries.
271	We can deploy LLMs with reduced dependencies by shipping the library for deployment without going through compilation.
272	It will also enable advanced options such as cross-compiling the libraries for web and mobile deployments.
273	
274	
275	Below is an example command of compiling model libraries in MLC LLM:
276	
277	.. code:: bash
278	
279	  export MODEL_LIB=$MLC_MODEL_PATH/lib.so  # ".dylib" for Intel Macs.
280	                                            # ".dll" for Windows.
281	                                            # ".wasm" for web.
282	                                            # ".tar" for iPhone/Android.
283	  mlc_llm compile $MLC_MODEL_PATH -o $MODEL_LIB
284	
285	At runtime, we need to specify this model library path to use it. For example,
286	
287	.. code:: bash
288	
289	  # For chat CLI
290	  mlc_llm chat $MLC_MODEL_PATH --model-lib $MODEL_LIB
291	  # For REST server
292	  mlc_llm serve $MLC_MODEL_PATH --model-lib $MODEL_LIB
293	
294	.. code:: python
295	
296	  from mlc_llm import MLCEngine
297	
298	  # For Python API
299	  model = "models/phi-2"
300	  model_lib = "models/phi-2/lib.so"
301	  engine = MLCEngine(model, model_lib=model_lib)
302	
303	:ref:`compile-model-libraries` introduces the model compilation command in detail,
304	where you can find instructions and example commands to compile model to different
305	hardware backends, such as WebGPU, iOS and Android.
306	
307	Universal Deployment
308	--------------------
309	
310	MLC LLM is a high-performance universal deployment solution for large language models,
311	to enable native deployment of any large language models with native APIs with compiler acceleration
312	So far, we have gone through several examples running on a local GPU environment.
313	The project supports multiple kinds of GPU backends.
314	
315	You can use `--device` option in compilation and runtime to pick a specific GPU backend.
316	For example, if you have an NVIDIA or AMD GPU, you can try to use the option below
317	to run chat through the vulkan backend. Vulkan-based LLM applications run in less typical
318	environments (e.g. SteamDeck).
319	
320	.. code:: bash
321	
322	    mlc_llm chat HF://mlc-ai/Llama-3-8B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC --device vulkan
323	
324	The same core LLM runtime engine powers all the backends, enabling the same model to be deployed across backends as
325	long as they fit within the memory and computing budget of the corresponding hardware backend.
326	We also leverage machine learning compilation to build backend-specialized optimizations to
327	get out the best performance on the targetted backend when possible, and reuse key insights and optimizations
328	across backends we support.
329	
330	Please checkout the what to do next sections below to find out more about different deployment scenarios,
331	such as WebGPU-based browser deployment, mobile and other settings.
332	
333	Summary and What to Do Next
334	---------------------------
335	
336	To briefly summarize this page,
337	
338	- We went through three examples (chat CLI, Python API, and REST server) of MLC LLM,
339	- we introduced how to convert model weights for your own models to run with MLC LLM, and (optionally) how to compile your models.
340	- We also discussed the universal deployment capability of MLC LLM.
341	
342	Next, please feel free to check out the pages below for quick start examples and more detailed information
343	on specific platforms
344	
345	- :ref:`Quick start examples <quick-start>` for Python API, chat CLI, REST server, web browser, iOS and Android.
346	- Depending on your use case, check out our API documentation and tutorial pages:
347	
348	  - :ref:`webllm-runtime`
349	  - :ref:`deploy-rest-api`
350	  - :ref:`deploy-cli`
351	  - :ref:`deploy-python-engine`
352	  - :ref:`deploy-ios`
353	  - :ref:`deploy-android`
354	  - :ref:`deploy-ide-integration`
355	
356	- :ref:`Convert model weight to MLC format <convert-weights-via-MLC>`, if you want to run your own models.
357	- :ref:`Compile model libraries <compile-model-libraries>`, if you want to deploy to web/iOS/Android or control the model optimizations.
358	- Report any problem or ask any question: open new issues in our `GitHub repo <https://github.com/mlc-ai/mlc-llm/issues>`_.
359

.. _introduction-to-mlc-llm:

Introduction to MLC LLM
=======================

.. contents:: Table of Contents
    :local:
    :depth: 2

MLC LLM is a machine learning compiler and high-performance deployment
engine for large language models.  The mission of this project is to enable everyone to develop,
optimize, and deploy AI models natively on everyone's platforms. 

This page is a quick tutorial to introduce how to try out MLC LLM, and the steps to
deploy your own models with MLC LLM.

Installation
------------

:ref:`MLC LLM <install-mlc-packages>` is available via pip.
It is always recommended to install it in an isolated conda virtual environment.

To verify the installation, activate your virtual environment, run

.. code:: bash

  python -c "import mlc_llm; print(mlc_llm.__path__)"

You are expected to see the installation path of MLC LLM Python package.


Chat CLI
--------

As the first example, we try out the chat CLI in MLC LLM with 4-bit quantized 8B Llama-3 model.
You can run MLC chat through a one-liner command:

.. code:: bash

    mlc_llm chat HF://mlc-ai/Llama-3-8B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC

It may take 1-2 minutes for the first time running this command.
After waiting, this command launch a chat interface where you can enter your prompt and chat with the model.

.. code::

  You can use the following special commands:
  /help               print the special commands
  /exit               quit the cli
  /stats              print out the latest stats (token/sec)
  /reset              restart a fresh chat
  /set [overrides]    override settings in the generation config. For example,
                        `/set temperature=0.5;max_gen_len=100;stop=end,stop`
                        Note: Separate stop words in the `stop` option with commas (,).
  Multi-line input: Use escape+enter to start a new line.

  user: What's the meaning of life
  assistant:
  What a profound and intriguing question! While there's no one definitive answer, I'd be happy to help you explore some perspectives on the meaning of life.

  The concept of the meaning of life has been debated and...


The figure below shows what run under the hood of this chat CLI command.
For the first time running the command, there are three major phases.

- **Phase 1. Pre-quantized weight download.** This phase automatically downloads pre-quantized Llama-3 model from `Hugging Face <https://huggingface.co/mlc-ai/Llama-3-8B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC>`_ and saves it to your local cache directory.
- **Phase 2. Model compilation.** This phase automatically optimizes the Llama-3 model to accelerate model inference on GPU with techniques of machine learning compilation in `Apache TVM <https://llm.mlc.ai/docs/install/tvm.html>`_ compiler, and generate the binary model library that enables the execution language models on your local GPU.
- **Phase 3. Chat runtime.** This phase consumes the model library built in phase 2 and the model weights downloaded in phase 1, launches a platform-native chat runtime to drive the execution of Llama-3 model.

We cache the pre-quantized model weights and compiled model library locally.
Therefore, phase 1 and 2 will only execute **once** over multiple runs.

.. figure:: /_static/img/project-workflow.svg
  :width: 700
  :align: center
  :alt: Project Workflow

  Workflow in MLC LLM

.. note::

  If you want to enable tensor parallelism to run LLMs on multiple GPUs,
  please specify argument ``--overrides "tensor_parallel_shards=$NGPU"``.
  For example,

  .. code:: shell

    mlc_llm chat HF://mlc-ai/Llama-3-8B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC --overrides "tensor_parallel_shards=2"

.. _introduction-to-mlc-llm-python-api:

Python API
----------

In the second example, we run the Llama-3 model with the chat completion Python API of MLC LLM.
You can save the code below into a Python file and run it.

.. code:: python

  from mlc_llm import MLCEngine

  # Create engine
  model = "HF://mlc-ai/Llama-3-8B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC"
  engine = MLCEngine(model)

  # Run chat completion in OpenAI API.
  for response in engine.chat.completions.create(
      messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What is the meaning of life?"}],
      model=model,
      stream=True,
  ):
      for choice in response.choices:
          print(choice.delta.content, end="", flush=True)
  print("\n")

  engine.terminate()

.. figure:: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mlc-ai/web-data/main/images/mlc-llm/tutorials/python-engine-api.jpg
  :width: 500
  :align: center

  MLC LLM Python API

This code example first creates an :class:`mlc_llm.MLCEngine` instance with the 4-bit quantized Llama-3 model.
**We design the Python API** :class:`mlc_llm.MLCEngine` **to align with OpenAI API**,
which means you can use :class:`mlc_llm.MLCEngine` in the same way of using
`OpenAI's Python package <https://github.com/openai/openai-python?tab=readme-ov-file#usage>`_
for both synchronous and asynchronous generation.

In this code example, we use the synchronous chat completion interface and iterate over
all the stream responses.
If you want to run without streaming, you can run

.. code:: python

  response = engine.chat.completions.create(
      messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What is the meaning of life?"}],
      model=model,
      stream=False,
  )
  print(response)

You can also try different arguments supported in `OpenAI chat completion API <https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create>`_.
If you would like to do concurrent asynchronous generation, you can use :class:`mlc_llm.AsyncMLCEngine` instead.

.. note::

  If you want to enable tensor parallelism to run LLMs on multiple GPUs,
  please specify argument ``model_config_overrides`` in MLCEngine constructor.
  For example,

  .. code:: python

    from mlc_llm import MLCEngine
    from mlc_llm.serve.config import EngineConfig

    model = "HF://mlc-ai/Llama-3-8B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC"
    engine = MLCEngine(
        model,
        engine_config=EngineConfig(tensor_parallel_shards=2),
    )


REST Server
-----------

For the third example, we launch a REST server to serve the 4-bit quantized Llama-3 model
for OpenAI chat completion requests. The server can be launched in command line with

.. code:: bash

  mlc_llm serve HF://mlc-ai/Llama-3-8B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC

The server is hooked at ``http://127.0.0.1:8000`` by default, and you can use ``--host`` and ``--port``
to set a different host and port.
When the server is ready (showing ``INFO: Uvicorn running on http://127.0.0.1:8000 (Press CTRL+C to quit)``),
we can open a new shell and send a cURL request via the following command:

.. code:: bash

  curl -X POST \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
          "model": "HF://mlc-ai/Llama-3-8B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC",
          "messages": [
              {"role": "user", "content": "Hello! Our project is MLC LLM. What is the name of our project?"}
          ]
    }' \
    http://127.0.0.1:8000/v1/chat/completions

The server will process this request and send back the response.
Similar to :ref:`introduction-to-mlc-llm-python-api`, you can pass argument ``"stream": true``
to request for stream responses.

.. note::

  If you want to enable tensor parallelism to run LLMs on multiple GPUs,
  please specify argument ``--overrides "tensor_parallel_shards=$NGPU"``.
  For example,

  .. code:: shell

    mlc_llm serve HF://mlc-ai/Llama-3-8B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC --overrides "tensor_parallel_shards=2"

.. _introduction-deploy-your-own-model:

Deploy Your Own Model
---------------------

So far we have been using pre-converted models weights from Hugging Face.
This section introduces the core workflow regarding how you can *run your own models with MLC LLM*.

We use the `Phi-2 <https://huggingface.co/microsoft/phi-2>`_ as the example model.
Assuming the Phi-2 model is downloaded and placed under ``models/phi-2``,
there are two major steps to prepare your own models.

- **Step 1. Generate MLC config.** The first step is to generate the configuration file of MLC LLM.

  .. code:: bash

    export LOCAL_MODEL_PATH=models/phi-2   # The path where the model resides locally.
    export MLC_MODEL_PATH=dist/phi-2-MLC/  # The path where to place the model processed by MLC.
    export QUANTIZATION=q0f16              # The choice of quantization.
    export CONV_TEMPLATE=phi-2             # The choice of conversation template.
    mlc_llm gen_config $LOCAL_MODEL_PATH \
        --quantization $QUANTIZATION \
        --conv-template $CONV_TEMPLATE \
        -o $MLC_MODEL_PATH

  The config generation command takes in the local model path, the target path of MLC output,
  the conversation template name in MLC and the quantization name in MLC.
  Here the quantization ``q0f16`` means float16 without quantization,
  and the conversation template ``phi-2`` is the Phi-2 model's template in MLC.

  If you want to enable tensor parallelism on multiple GPUs, add argument
  ``--tensor-parallel-shards $NGPU`` to the config generation command.

  - `The full list of supported quantization in MLC <https://github.com/mlc-ai/mlc-llm/blob/main/python/mlc_llm/quantization/quantization.py#L29>`_. You can try different quantization methods with MLC LLM. Typical quantization methods are ``q4f16_1`` for 4-bit group quantization, ``q4f16_ft`` for 4-bit FasterTransformer format quantization.
  - `The full list of conversation template in MLC <https://github.com/mlc-ai/mlc-llm/blob/main/python/mlc_llm/interface/gen_config.py#L276>`_.

- **Step 2. Convert model weights.** In this step, we convert the model weights to MLC format.

  .. code:: bash

    mlc_llm convert_weight $LOCAL_MODEL_PATH \
      --quantization $QUANTIZATION \
      -o $MLC_MODEL_PATH

  This step consumes the raw model weights and converts them to for MLC format.
  The converted weights will be stored under ``$MLC_MODEL_PATH``,
  which is the same directory where the config file generated in Step 1 resides.

Now, we can try to run your own model with chat CLI:

.. code:: bash

  mlc_llm chat $MLC_MODEL_PATH

For the first run, model compilation will be triggered automatically to optimize the
model for GPU accelerate and generate the binary model library.
The chat interface will be displayed after model JIT compilation finishes.
You can also use this model in Python API, MLC serve and other use scenarios.

(Optional) Compile Model Library
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

In previous sections, model libraries are compiled when the :class:`mlc_llm.MLCEngine` launches,
which is what we call "JIT (Just-in-Time) model compilation".
In some cases, it is beneficial to explicitly compile the model libraries.
We can deploy LLMs with reduced dependencies by shipping the library for deployment without going through compilation.
It will also enable advanced options such as cross-compiling the libraries for web and mobile deployments.


Below is an example command of compiling model libraries in MLC LLM:

.. code:: bash

  export MODEL_LIB=$MLC_MODEL_PATH/lib.so  # ".dylib" for Intel Macs.
                                            # ".dll" for Windows.
                                            # ".wasm" for web.
                                            # ".tar" for iPhone/Android.
  mlc_llm compile $MLC_MODEL_PATH -o $MODEL_LIB

At runtime, we need to specify this model library path to use it. For example,

.. code:: bash

  # For chat CLI
  mlc_llm chat $MLC_MODEL_PATH --model-lib $MODEL_LIB
  # For REST server
  mlc_llm serve $MLC_MODEL_PATH --model-lib $MODEL_LIB

.. code:: python

  from mlc_llm import MLCEngine

  # For Python API
  model = "models/phi-2"
  model_lib = "models/phi-2/lib.so"
  engine = MLCEngine(model, model_lib=model_lib)

:ref:`compile-model-libraries` introduces the model compilation command in detail,
where you can find instructions and example commands to compile model to different
hardware backends, such as WebGPU, iOS and Android.

Universal Deployment
--------------------

MLC LLM is a high-performance universal deployment solution for large language models,
to enable native deployment of any large language models with native APIs with compiler acceleration
So far, we have gone through several examples running on a local GPU environment.
The project supports multiple kinds of GPU backends.

You can use `--device` option in compilation and runtime to pick a specific GPU backend.
For example, if you have an NVIDIA or AMD GPU, you can try to use the option below
to run chat through the vulkan backend. Vulkan-based LLM applications run in less typical
environments (e.g. SteamDeck).

.. code:: bash

    mlc_llm chat HF://mlc-ai/Llama-3-8B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC --device vulkan

The same core LLM runtime engine powers all the backends, enabling the same model to be deployed across backends as
long as they fit within the memory and computing budget of the corresponding hardware backend.
We also leverage machine learning compilation to build backend-specialized optimizations to
get out the best performance on the targetted backend when possible, and reuse key insights and optimizations
across backends we support.

Please checkout the what to do next sections below to find out more about different deployment scenarios,
such as WebGPU-based browser deployment, mobile and other settings.

Summary and What to Do Next
---------------------------

To briefly summarize this page,

- We went through three examples (chat CLI, Python API, and REST server) of MLC LLM,
- we introduced how to convert model weights for your own models to run with MLC LLM, and (optionally) how to compile your models.
- We also discussed the universal deployment capability of MLC LLM.

Next, please feel free to check out the pages below for quick start examples and more detailed information
on specific platforms

- :ref:`Quick start examples <quick-start>` for Python API, chat CLI, REST server, web browser, iOS and Android.
- Depending on your use case, check out our API documentation and tutorial pages:

  - :ref:`webllm-runtime`
  - :ref:`deploy-rest-api`
  - :ref:`deploy-cli`
  - :ref:`deploy-python-engine`
  - :ref:`deploy-ios`
  - :ref:`deploy-android`
  - :ref:`deploy-ide-integration`

- :ref:`Convert model weight to MLC format <convert-weights-via-MLC>`, if you want to run your own models.
- :ref:`Compile model libraries <compile-model-libraries>`, if you want to deploy to web/iOS/Android or control the model optimizations.
- Report any problem or ask any question: open new issues in our `GitHub repo <https://github.com/mlc-ai/mlc-llm/issues>`_.

1	.. _compile-model-libraries:
2	
3	Compile Model Libraries
4	=======================
5	
6	To run a model with MLC LLM in any platform, we need:
7	
8	1. **Model weights** converted to MLC format (e.g. `RedPajama-INCITE-Chat-3B-v1-q4f16_1-MLC <https://huggingface.co/mlc-ai/RedPajama-INCITE-Chat-3B-v1-q4f16_1-MLC/tree/main>`__.)
9	2. **Model library** that comprises the inference logic
10	
11	This page describes how to compile a model library with MLC LLM. Model compilation optimizes
12	the model inference for a given platform, allowing users bring their own new model
13	architecture, use different quantization modes, and customize the overall model
14	optimization flow.
15	
16	
17	
18	Notably, in many cases you do not need to explicit call compile.
19	
20	- If you are using the Python API, you can skip specifying ``model_lib`` and
21	  the system will JIT compile the library.
22	
23	- If you are building iOS/android package, checkout :ref:`package-libraries-and-weights`,
24	  which provides a simpler high-level command that leverages the compile behind the scheme.
25	
26	
27	This page is still helpful to understand the compilation flow behind the scheme,
28	or be used to explicit create model libraries.
29	We compile ``RedPajama-INCITE-Chat-3B-v1`` with ``q4f16_1`` as an example for all platforms.
30	
31	.. note::
32	    Before you proceed, make sure you followed :ref:`install-tvm`, a required
33	    backend to compile models with MLC LLM.
34	
35	    Please also follow the instructions in :ref:`deploy-cli` / :ref:`deploy-python-engine` to obtain
36	    the CLI app / Python API that can be used to chat with the compiled model.
37	
38	
39	.. contents:: Table of Contents
40	    :depth: 1
41	    :local:
42	
43	1. Verify Installation
44	----------------------
45	
46	**Step 1. Verify mlc_llm**
47	
48	We use the python package ``mlc_llm`` to compile models. This can be installed by
49	following :ref:`install-mlc-packages`, either by building from source, or by
50	installing the prebuilt package. Verify ``mlc_llm`` installation in command line via:
51	
52	.. code:: bash
53	
54	    $ mlc_llm --help
55	    # You should see help information with this line
56	    usage: MLC LLM Command Line Interface. [-h] {compile,convert_weight,gen_config}
57	
58	.. note::
59	    If it runs into error ``command not found: mlc_llm``, try ``python -m mlc_llm --help``.
60	
61	**Step 2. Verify TVM**
62	
63	To compile models, you also need to follow :ref:`install-tvm`.
64	Here we verify ``tvm`` quickly with command line (for full verification, see :ref:`tvm-validate`):
65	
66	.. code:: bash
67	
68	    $ python -c "import tvm; print(tvm.__file__)"
69	    /some-path/lib/python3.13/site-packages/tvm/__init__.py
70	
71	1. Clone from HF and convert_weight
72	-----------------------------------
73	
74	This replicates :ref:`convert-weights-via-MLC`, see that page for more details.
75	
76	You can be under the mlc-llm repo, or your own working directory. Note that all platforms
77	can share the same compiled/quantized weights.
78	
79	.. code:: shell
80	
81	    # Create directory
82	    mkdir -p dist/models && cd dist/models
83	    # Clone HF weights
84	    git lfs install
85	    git clone https://huggingface.co/togethercomputer/RedPajama-INCITE-Chat-3B-v1
86	    cd ../..
87	    # Convert weight
88	    mlc_llm convert_weight ./dist/models/RedPajama-INCITE-Chat-3B-v1/ \
89	        --quantization q4f16_1 \
90	        -o dist/RedPajama-INCITE-Chat-3B-v1-q4f16_1-MLC
91	
92	2. Generate mlc-chat-config and compile
93	---------------------------------------
94	
95	A model library is specified by:
96	
97	 - The model architecture (e.g. ``llama-2``, ``gpt-neox``)
98	 - Quantization (e.g. ``q4f16_1``, ``q0f32``)
99	 - Metadata (e.g. ``context_window_size``, ``sliding_window_size``, ``prefill-chunk-size``), which affects memory planning
100	 - Platform (e.g. ``cuda``, ``webgpu``, ``iOS``)
101	
102	All these knobs are specified in ``mlc-chat-config.json`` generated by ``gen_config``.
103	
104	.. code:: shell
105	
106	    # Create output directory for the model library compiled
107	    mkdir dist/libs
108	
109	.. tabs::
110	
111	    .. group-tab:: Linux - CUDA
112	
113	        .. code:: shell
114	
115	            # 1. gen_config: generate mlc-chat-config.json and process tokenizers
116	            mlc_llm gen_config ./dist/models/RedPajama-INCITE-Chat-3B-v1/ \
117	                --quantization q4f16_1 --conv-template redpajama_chat \
118	                -o dist/RedPajama-INCITE-Chat-3B-v1-q4f16_1-MLC/
119	            # 2. compile: compile model library with specification in mlc-chat-config.json
120	            mlc_llm compile ./dist/RedPajama-INCITE-Chat-3B-v1-q4f16_1-MLC/mlc-chat-config.json \
121	                --device cuda -o dist/libs/RedPajama-INCITE-Chat-3B-v1-q4f16_1-cuda.so
122

440	Compile Commands for More Models
441	--------------------------------
442	
443	This section lists compile commands for more models that you can try out. Note that this can be easily
444	generalized to any model variant, as long as mlc-llm supports the architecture.
445	
446	.. tabs::
447	
448	    .. tab:: Model: Llama-2-7B
449	
450	        Please `request for access <https://huggingface.co/meta-llama>`_ to the Llama-2 weights from Meta first.
451	        After granted access, first create directory ``dist/models`` and download the model to the directory.
452	        For example, you can run the following code:
453	
454	        .. code:: shell
455	
456	            mkdir -p dist/models && cd dist/models
457	            git lfs install
458	            git clone https://huggingface.co/meta-llama/Llama-2-7b-chat-hf
459	            cd ../..
460	
461	        Then convert the HF weights into MLC-compatible weights. Note that all platforms
462	        can share the same compiled/quantized weights.
463	
464	        .. code:: shell
465	
466	            mlc_llm convert_weight ./dist/models/Llama-2-7b-chat-hf/ --quantization q4f16_1 -o dist/Llama-2-7b-chat-hf-q4f16_1-MLC
467	
468	        Afterwards, run the following command to generate mlc config and compile the model.
469	
470	        .. code:: shell
471	
472	            # Create output directory for the model library compiled
473	            mkdir dist/libs
474	
475	        .. tabs::
476	
477	            .. tab:: Target: CUDA
478	
479	                .. code:: shell
480	
481	                    # 1. gen_config: generate mlc-chat-config.json and process tokenizers
482	                    mlc_llm gen_config ./dist/models/Llama-2-7b-chat-hf/ --quantization q4f16_1 \
483	                        --conv-template llama-2 -o dist/Llama-2-7b-chat-hf-q4f16_1-MLC/
484	                    # 2. compile: compile model library with specification in mlc-chat-config.json
485	                    mlc_llm compile ./dist/Llama-2-7b-chat-hf-q4f16_1-MLC/mlc-chat-config.json \
486	                        --device cuda -o dist/libs/Llama-2-7b-chat-hf-q4f16_1-cuda.so
487	
488	            .. tab:: Metal
489	
490	                For M-chip Mac:
491	
492	                .. code:: shell
493	
494	                    # 1. gen_config: generate mlc-chat-config.json and process tokenizers
495	                    mlc_llm gen_config ./dist/models/Llama-2-7b-chat-hf/ --quantization q4f16_1 \
496	                        --conv-template llama-2 -o dist/Llama-2-7b-chat-hf-q4f16_1-MLC/
497	                    # 2. compile: compile model library with specification in mlc-chat-config.json
498	                    mlc_llm compile ./dist/Llama-2-7b-chat-hf-q4f16_1-MLC/mlc-chat-config.json \
499	                        --device metal -o dist/libs/Llama-2-7b-chat-hf-q4f16_1-metal.so

591	    .. tab:: Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2
592	
593	        Note that Mistral uses sliding window attention (SWA). Thus, instead of specifying
594	        ``context-window-size``, we specify ``sliding-window-size``.
595	
596	        First create directory ``dist/models`` and download the model to the directory.
597	        For example, you can run the following code:
598	
599	        .. code:: shell
600	
601	            mkdir -p dist/models && cd dist/models
602	            git lfs install
603	            git clone https://huggingface.co/mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2
604	            cd ../..
605	
606	        Then convert the HF weights into MLC-compatible weights. Note that all platforms
607	        can share the same compiled/quantized weights.
608	
609	        .. code:: shell
610	
611	            mlc_llm convert_weight ./dist/models/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2/ --quantization q4f16_1 \
612	                -o dist/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2-q4f16_1-MLC
